model_narrative: We are planning a closed route that visits a collection of cities. For every ordered pair of cities we decide whether the route travels directly from the first to the second. Each direct leg between two cities has a known travel cost, and the goal is to choose the legs so that the total travel cost of the route is as small as possible.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the cities, treated as origins; each city is a place the tour passes through

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the cities, treated as destinations; this is the same collection of cities as the origin set, used to name where an arc lands

params.name: c
params.doc: the cost of travelling directly from the origin city to the destination city, in distance units; the cost from a city to itself is zero

vars.name: x
vars.doc: a binary indicator that is one when the route goes directly from the origin city to the destination city and zero otherwise; the entries from a city to itself are held at zero so no city links to itself

description: The route must depart from every city exactly once. For each origin city, exactly one direct leg leaving that city to some destination city is chosen.

model_narrative: We are planning a closed route that visits a collection of cities. For every ordered pair of cities we decide whether the route travels directly from the first to the second. Each direct leg between two cities has a known travel cost, and the goal is to choose the legs so that the total travel cost of the route is as small as possible.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the cities, treated as origins; each city is a place the tour passes through

sets.name: j

vars.name: x
vars.doc: a binary indicator that is one when the route goes directly from the origin city to the destination city and zero otherwise; the entries from a city to itself are held at zero so no city links to itself

description: The route must arrive at every city exactly once. For each destination city, exactly one direct leg coming into that city from some origin city is chosen.
